headline: The Dow Jones May Be Down, But DOW Stock Is Winning in 2026
summary: Dow (DOW) is a leading material science company with a broad, differentiated product portfolio and global manufacturing footprint. DOW has demonstrated strong technical momentum, trading at a new 52-week high. Plus, the stock pays a dividend yielding a generous 3.32%. However, further upside may be limited. headline: Here Are The Stock Market’s Biggest Winners This Month
summary: It's been a pretty rough month for stocks. But all those wild swings have minted some winners—most of them, unsurprisingly, in the energy sector. Here are some of the best-performing tickers this month, as of Tuesday morning trading:    Energy exchange-traded funds: The United States Oil Fund LP is up about 59% in March, its best monthly performance on record.

headline: S&P 500 Shows Some Surprise Winners After One Month Of War
summary: The war in Iran defined U.S. stock market behavior in March. While oil and natural gas names seized the bulk of the largest gains among S&P 500 stocks, two of the top-five advancers — Dow Inc. (DOW) and LyondellBasell (LYB) — were maybe less expected. Over the course of this month through Monday, Dow rose 36% and LyondellBasell gained 43%,...

headline: Why Dow (DOW) Is Up 16.2% After Warning On Petrochemical Shortages And Inflationary Pressures
summary: In March 2026, Dow Inc. highlighted how petrochemical shortages linked to Middle East tensions were affecting input costs across construction, consumer goods, automotive, and aerospace industries, with its CEO warning of inflationary pressure through the end of the year. The comments pointed to a period where tight supply could lift petrochemical prices and potentially support Dow’s profit margins, even as customers face higher costs. Next, we’ll examine how expectations for tighter...

headline: Dow Inc. (DOW) Jumps 2.6% on Petrochem Shortage
summary: Dow Inc. (NYSE:DOW) is one of the 10 Stocks Leaving the Market in the Dust Today. Dow Inc saw its share prices increase by 2.57 percent on Monday to close at $41.87 apiece, as investors digested news about the petrochemical shortage amid the ongoing tensions in the Middle East.
